questions.question: The payment of residential utility bills is a duty of the
questions.explanation: Local governments are responsible for utility bills of residents.

questions.question: Standards, rule and criteria that determine acceptable norms that influence a cordial relationship in the society are referred to as
questions.explanation: Values influence norms and acceptable behavior.

questions.question: A social affiliation between two or more people in the society is known as
questions.explanation: Interpersonal relations involve social connections between individuals.

questions.question: A major achievement that can be attributed to good intercommunal relationship in Nigeria is

options.A: encouraging competition among individuals in the society
options.C: promoting peace, mutual understanding and development

questions.explanation: Good intercommunal relations promote peace and development.

questions.question: A person born in Ghana by Nigerian parents and resides in Ghana is a citizen of
questions.explanation: Born in Ghana, so Ghanaian citizen.

questions.question: God-fatherism in a democratic system of government can lead to
questions.explanation: God-fatherism often leads to favoritism and neglect of others.

questions.question: Road signs are usually
questions.explanation: Road signs mainly inform and regulate.

questions.question: Execution and implementation of government policies are parts of the functions of the
questions.explanation: Public service executes government policies.

questions.question: Right to ownership of property falls under the category of
questions.explanation: Ownership rights are civil rights.

questions.question: The Nigerian Tribune newspaper was established by
questions.explanation: Obafemi Awolowo founded the Nigerian Tribune.

questions.question: The right to freedom of expression can be limited by
questions.explanation: Slander can limit freedom of expression.

questions.question: Local government administration promotes
questions.explanation: LG promotes democracy at local levels.

questions.question: When voters exercise their right to vote based on monetary or material inducements, the elected candidates may suffer

options.A: loss of voters support in the next election
options.D: loss of party support for another term

questions.explanation: Vote-buying undermines legitimacy.

questions.question: The unpleasant attitudes towards people Living With HIV/AIDS is termed
questions.explanation: Stigmatization refers to negative attitudes.

questions.question: Human rights abuse can be prevented with the existence of
questions.explanation: Judicial independence helps prevent abuse.

questions.question: Traffic regulations are law that mainly

options.A: guide all road users on safety practices
options.B: instruct drivers on the use of traffic signs only
options.C: ensure the teaching of road safety in school
options.D: establish agencies for control of traffic

questions.explanation: Traffic laws govern safety for all users.

questions.question: The search of a citizen's residence by the police without a warrant or court order amounts to a breach of his/her right to
questions.explanation: Warrantless search breaches privacy rights.

questions.question: Legislations on issues affecting policies on money, policing and international relations are within the scope of
questions.explanation: Money, policing, and international relations are shared legislative areas.

questions.question: The Nigerian federation is divided into how many geopolitical zones?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has six geopolitical zones.

questions.question: The traffic sign indicated in Figure 1 means
questions.explanation: Figure 1 shows a no crossing sign.

questions.question: Where you will likely not find the sign in Figure 1 in Nigeria is
questions.explanation: No crossing signs are common in official or secure zones, less common in malls.

questions.question: Under the Nigeria 1999 constitution as amended, the right to secede is

options.B: allowed to any state in the federation
options.C: resident in local government in federation
options.D: denied to all states in the federation

questions.explanation: Secession is not constitutionally permitted.

questions.question: The major aim of youth empowerment programmes it to

options.A: tackle the problem of insecurity and lawlessness
options.B: provide vocational skills to the youths
options.C: help the youths become good decision makers
options.D: provide attitudinal reorientation to the youths

questions.explanation: Empowerment aims at skills development.

questions.question:

The major reasons for citizens' reorientation in civic education are
questions.explanation: Reorientation involves awareness of rights and responsibilities.

questions.question: One of the political responsibilities of a citizen is
questions.explanation: Voting is a key political responsibility.

questions.question: Authority that is recognised by the law of the land is called
questions.explanation: Lawfully recognized authority is constituted authority.

questions.question: Traffic regulations are mainly meant for all
questions.explanation: Traffic laws cover all road users.

questions.question: A major attribute an individual will likely exhibit if not contented is
questions.explanation: Discontent may lead to greed.

questions.question: To enhance national development, parenthood which is the nucleus of every society must be

options.C: a civil and morally responsible one

questions.explanation: Responsible parenthood promotes development.

questions.question: The beam of yellow colour on a traffic light is an indication to drivers to
questions.explanation: Yellow indicates caution and readiness to move or stop.

questions.question: One significance of democracy in Nigeria is that it promotes
questions.explanation: Democracy promotes constitutional governance.

questions.question: When power is concentrated in one central government, the constitution is said to be
questions.explanation: Unitarian systems centralize power.

questions.question: When opposing communities come together, to discuss a way out of a pending feud, the action is best described as
questions.explanation: Arbitration involves settling disputes.

questions.question: Positive interpersonal relationship mainly promotes
questions.explanation: Good relationships promote peace.

questions.question: Political apathy can be addressed by

options.B: selling votes to the highest bidders
options.D: criticizing the policies of elected officials

questions.explanation: Awareness campaigns reduce apathy.

questions.question: The executive, legislative and judiciary constitute the
questions.explanation: The three branches form the arms of government.

questions.question: The machinery through which the will of the state is driven is described as
questions.explanation: Government executes the will of the state.

questions.question: Which of the following is important for sustainable human relation in a society?
questions.explanation: Tolerance fosters peaceful coexistence.

questions.question: Human trafficking can be described as

options.A: exploitation of humans in ways their rights are deprived in the course of movement
options.B: exploration and adventurers in foreign countries
options.C: recruitment of youths as casual workers in distant places
options.D: desperate desire by youths to travel abroad for greener pasture

questions.explanation: Human trafficking involves exploitation.

questions.question: Which of the following values will likely, cause harm to the society?
questions.explanation: Indiscipline harms societal harmony.

questions.question: Civil societies act mainly as a link between
questions.explanation: Civil societies connect the government and citizens.

questions.question: Legislative arm of government can check the executive by

options.A: demanding the review of any act of the executive
options.C: reporting them to the supreme court

questions.explanation: Legislature can check the executive through oversight.

questions.question: Which of the following factors have made political apathy in Nigeria more pronounced?

options.A: Long period of dictatorship and election rigging
options.B: Indiscriminate registration of parties and illiteracy
options.D: Use of card readers and complexity of voting procedure

questions.explanation: Poverty and ethnicity contribute to apathy.

questions.question: Use the write-up below to answer the question. Driving on the highways entails being very watchful, observant and attentive to one's surrounding, else you may run into an object or someone. Most accidents are caused by human factors.
questions.explanation: Careful driving prevents accidents.

questions.question: Combating human trafficking requires the efforts of

options.A: government, educational bodies, religious organizations excluding civil services
options.B: international bodies, pressure groups and other non-governmental agencies only
options.C: individuals, government, non-governmental agencies including international bodies
options.D: government and non-governmental agencies excluding the students

questions.explanation: All sectors must collaborate.

questions.question: Activities of secret cults are not likely to be prominent in
questions.explanation: Secret cult activities are less visible in open markets.

questions.question: An inalienable right enjoyed by citizens of a state but denied all non-citizens is the right to
questions.explanation: Voting rights are typically inalienable for citizens.
